title: pedicularis racemosa var. alba
intro: Pedicularis racemosa var. alba, also known as White Lousewort or Western Indian Paintbrush, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of western North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Pedicularis racemosa var. alba include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
